steps.title: Factor out GCD first
steps.content:
The expression $7290x^4 - 1440$ shares a common factor of 90. Factoring this out: $$7290x^4 - 1440 = 90(81x^4 - 16)$$
This reduces the problem to analyzing $81x^4 - 16$.

steps.title: Difference of squares decomposition
steps.content:
Recognize $81x^4$ as $(9x^2)^2$ and $16$ as $4^2$. Apply the difference of squares formula: $$81x^4 - 16 = (9x^2)^2 - 4^2 = (9x^2 - 4)(9x^2 + 4)$$
Now $9x^2 - 4$ can be further factored as $$(9x^2 - 4) = (3x - 2)(3x + 2)$$
Full factorization: $$90(3x - 2)(3x + 2)(9x^2 + 4)$$

steps.title: Compare to answer choices
steps.content: The factor $9x^{2}+4$ matches one factor, and $3x+2$ and $90$ are also directly present. The expression $3x^{2}-2$ never appears in the valid factorization. B is correct.
